mission.title: pushgateway: Label values containing '/' return HTTP 400
mission.description: When pushing metrics to the Pushgateway with label values containing '/', the request returns HTTP 400. This is caused by Go's URL parser unescaping %2F before the router sees the path, making it impossible to distinguish path separators from literal slashes in label values. Resolved in Pushgateway v1.0+ with base64-encoded label value support.

steps.title: Understand the root cause
steps.description:
The Pushgateway uses URL path segments to identify job and label values (e.g., `/metrics/job/myjob/instance/myinstance`). When a label value contains `/`, it gets URL-encoded as `%2F`, but Go's `net/http` unescapes it before routing — so the router interprets it as a path separator.

This is a fundamental limitation of Go's HTTP libraries, not a Pushgateway bug.

steps.title: Upgrade to Pushgateway v1.0+ for base64 support
steps.description:
Starting with v1.0.0, the Pushgateway supports base64-encoded label values as a workaround. Prefix the value with `@base64` and provide the base64-encoded value:
```bash
# Encode the label value containing '/'
echo -n 'path/with/slashes' | base64
# Output: cGF0aC93aXRoL3NsYXNoZXM=

# Push using base64-encoded value
curl -s --data-binary 'some_metric 42' \
  'http://localhost:9091/metrics/job/myjob/instance@base64/cGF0aC93aXRoL3NsYXNoZXM='
```

steps.title: Verify the workaround
steps.description:
Confirm that the metric was pushed correctly with the decoded label value:
```bash
curl -s http://localhost:9091/metrics | grep some_metric
```
You should see the metric with the original label value `path/with/slashes` (decoded from base64).

resolution.summary: Pushgateway v1.0+ supports base64-encoded label values to work around Go's URL parser unescaping %2F. Prefix the label name with @base64 and provide the base64-encoded value in the URL path. This allows label values containing '/' and other special characters.

prerequisites.description: A running Prometheus Pushgateway v1.0+ deployment (typically in a monitoring namespace).
